name: Alytus County (Lithuania)

colors.symbolism: the standard outer field of Lithuanian county flags
the field of the county coat of arms

description: A blue field with an inner black panel set off by a narrow white-and-yellow border, bearing the coat of arms of Alytus County in the centre.
history: Like the flags of all ten Lithuanian counties, the flag of Alytus County follows a uniform template introduced after the country's redivision into apskritys (counties) in 1994: a blue outer border surrounding the county's heraldic field with the county arms at the centre.

features.symbolism: blue border in the Lithuanian county pattern, with a black inner field referring to the county arms

features.role: coat of arms of Alytus County
features.symbolism: an armoured knight on horseback charging with raised sword on a shield, derived from the historic Vytis but in the colours of Alytus
